title: VP of Software Engineering
description:
VP of Software Engineering
Canada, Remote
Apply

About CoLab

At CoLab, we help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner.

CoLab is the AI platform for driving stronger engineering decisions. Every design review in CoLab builds a knowledge repository of design feedback, decisions, and lessons learned - which AI agents draw from to flag issues on future designs before they compound. The more your team works in CoLab, the smarter it gets and the faster you arrive at the ideal design. Companies like Ford, Komatsu, and Johnson Controls use CoLab to catch issues earlier, eliminate rework cycles, and bring products to market faster.

Founded in St. John’s, Newfoundland, CoLab has grown quickly from our first customer in 2019 to a rapidly scaling company. We’ve recently been recognized on Deloitte’s Fast 50™ and Fast 500™, and named a Canadian company to watch by The Globe and Mail and Financial Post. 

About the Role

This role is for a high-impact engineering leader who has been in the trenches at fast-scaling startups — someone who has grown from individual contributor to leader and helped scale engineering orgs through multiple phases of growth. Ideally, you've done it more than once.

As VP of Software Engineering, you'll be responsible for building and maintaining a high-performing, scalable engineering organization across Product Development, Ops, and Technical Support. This is a role for someone who’s hands-on enough to spot an issue i

About the Role

CoLab is seeking a Principal Developer to join our growing team. 

In this high-impact, hybrid role, you will work closely with our leadership team as a senior Individual Contributor (IC) and product thinker. Your primary focus will be on supporting strategic initiatives from a technical perspective.

You will help define our product roadmap by building working prototypes, pressure-testing initial assumptions, proposing potential approaches, and transforming technical unknowns into clear, actionable product direction.

About the Role

This role involves building and scaling one of the most strategically important product development areas in CoLab: our AI/ML teams.

You’ll lead three teams: Applied ML, AutoReview, and Operator - bringing them into a cohesive, high-performing product area. This group is operating with startup-level speed and ambiguity - in this role you will be responsible for keeping the pace while adding clarity, structure, and consistent execution. Success here looks like consistently shipping meaningful, reliable AI capabilities,

title: Engineering Manager
description:
Engineering Manager
Canada, Remote
Apply
About CoLab

At CoLab, we want to help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner.

CoLab is a cloud based platform for engineering design review. We make it easy for subject matter experts (SMEs) across your business to access, evaluate, and comment on 2D drawings and 3D models. Our built-in AI peer checker, AutoReview, scans designs for common errors or non-compliance with your standards and guidelines. AutoReview creates markups and comments on your files, in context – just like a human checker.

With CoLab, human SMEs and AI work together to help you make better decisions and improve designs faster. We automatically capture knowledge from across your global business that would otherwise be buried in emails, spreadsheets, slide decks, and unknown locations in Sharepoint or PLM. Then, we make sure every lesson learned and every design guideline is applied exactly when it matters.

Companies like Johnson Controls, Komatsu, Schaeffler, and Polaris have launched products 40% faster, cut BOM costs by 50%, and reduced quality escapes by 15% in 1 year.

About the Role

We’re hiring an Engineering Manager to lead Build Operator, one of the most strategically important product areas at CoLab.

Operator is our conversational AI agent for engineering teams. It helps users search engineering knowledge, understand complex design data, execute actions inside CoLab, and automate review workflows. 

A lot of th

About the Role
CoLab’s AutoReview product is a core part of how we bring AI and engineering expertise together to improve design quality. So far, much of that work has focused on 2D drawings. Now, we’re building the future of 3D AutoReview — and we need a backend engineer who’s excited to help in that mission.

You’ll join the AutoReview team and help build the systems that power intelligent 3D design analysis at scale. That includes everything from geometry parsing and model analysis to backend infrastructure, rule

Sales Engineering at CoLab is a team of mechanical engineers who sit at the intersection of product, sales, and customer success. They act as product experts, industry experts, and the voice of the customer—playing a critical role in helping prospects and customers realize the full value of CoLab.

The team operates across three core functions:

Sales Engineers (SEs): Focus on the pre-sales experience, partnering with prospective customers to evaluate CoLab and understand how it fits into their workflows and engineering challenges.

Chief Financial Officer
Industry
Executive
Accounting & Finance
Location
Nova Scotia
APPLY NOW
For more than 60 years, Northwood has been a powerful advocate for seniors and a leader in continuing care across Nova Scotia. Established as a social movement grounded in dignity, belonging, and choice, Northwood has grown into Atlantic Canada’s largest not for profit continuing care organization, serving more than 7,000 people through long-term care, home care, retirement living, and innovative community-based programs.

Northwood has been recognized for leading change in continuing care, advancing innovative models, embracing technology, and prioritizing dignity, choice, and belonging. Built on values of accountability, social justice, respect, and continuous improvement, Northwood remains deeply focused on people and ensuring individuals and families receive high-quality, compassionate care.

The Opportunity

Northwood is seeking a strategic, experienced, and mission-aligned Chief Financial Officer (CFO) to provide executive financial leadership within a large, complex, multi-site continuing care organization. Reporting to the President and CEO, the CFO is a key member of the senior leadership team and plays a central role in

Yorkville University is actively seeking its next Chair, Faculty of Education, to provide academic leadership and operational oversight for programs within the Faculty, including the Master of Education in Educational Leadership. This is a compelling opportunity to lead nationally relevant programs, influence academic quality and growth, and contribute meaningfully to a university committed to access, flexibility, and student success.

Yorkville University
At Yorkville University and Toronto Film School, we believe education is more than the pursuit of knowledge – it is a catalyst for transformation. Our mission, grounded in democratizing education, is to create access for learners from every background and empower them to achieve their aspirations and advance their careers.

We are committed to delivering rigorous, flexible programs that are both personally fulfilling and professionally rewarding, while contributing to the betterment of society. Operations

Nova Scotia
APPLY NOW
Ashburn Golf Club is one of Atlantic Canada’s premier private clubs, combining over a century of tradition with a modern and engaging member experience across Halifax and Fall River. Established in 1922 and designed by renowned Canadian architect Stanley Thompson, Ashburn is known for its championship-calibre golf and strong, community-oriented culture.

With two courses in Halifax and Fall River, along with dining, events, and year-round programming, the Club offers a dynamic and well-rounded experience supported by an engaged membership and a strong governance model.

As the Club enters an exciting period of growth, Ashburn Golf Club is seeking a strategic and engaging General Manager (GM) to lead the organization through this next phase and ensure continued operational excellence and long-term success.

What does this role entail?

Reporting to the Board of Directors, you will be responsible for setting and executing the Club’s strategic direction, with full accountability for financial performance, operations, and member experience. You will lead all core functions, including golf operations, food and beverage, facilities, membership services, and admi

Yorkville University
At Yorkville University and Toronto Film School, we believe education is more than the pursuit of knowledge – it is a catalyst for transformation. Our mission, grounded in democratizing education, is to create access for learners from every background and empower them to achieve their aspirations and advance their careers.

We are committed to delivering rigorous, flexible programs that are both personally fulfilling and professionally rewarding, while contributing to the betterment of society. Since 2004, Yorkville has been among Canada’s few private, for-profit, fully accredited degree-granting universities, with campuses in Ontario, British Columbia, and New Brunswick. Yorkville University is actively seeking its next Associate Dean of Business, Ontario – a senior academic leader who will play a pivotal role in shaping innovative, career-focused business education while advancing student success, faculty excellence, and program quality within a dynamic, growth-oriented institution.

Yorkville University
At Yorkville University and Toronto Film School, we believe education is more than the pursuit of knowledge – it is a catalyst for transformation. Our mission, grounded in democratizing education, is to create access for learners from every background and empower them to achieve their aspirations and advance their careers.

We are committed to delivering rigorous, flexible programs that are both personally fulfilling and professionally rewarding, while contributing to the betterment of society. Since 2004, Yorkville has been among Canada’s few private, for-profit, fully accredited degree-granting universities, with campuses in Ontario, British Columbia, and New Brunswick. Together with Toronto Film School, we offer creative and academic pathways at both the college and university levels, cultivatin

This is a pivotal opportunity to strengthen academic excellence, deepen community partnerships, and position Sault College as a resilient and trusted anchor institution for Sault Ste. Marie and Northern Ontario.

Sault College
Sault College has a proud history characterized by tremendous growth and spanning over more than five decades, evolving from a Vocational Centre opening in 1965 to the College it is today. Each year, the College responds to the postsecondary educational needs of more than 4,000 part-time and full-time students and continues to add new programs across all disciplines. Sault College currently offers 57 programs including 16 College Certificates; 29 Ontario College Diplomas; 4 Ontario College Advanced Diplomas; 6 Ontario Graduate Certificates; and 2 Bachelor’s Degrees. West Hants Regional Municipality is seeking an accomplished fire services professional and people-focused leader to serve as its next Director of Fire Services.

West Hants Regional Municipality

West Hants Regional Municipality (WHRM) is a dynamic and growing community in Hants County, Nova Scotia, formed through the amalgamation of the former Town of Windsor and the Municipality of the District of West Hants in 2020. Strategically situated along the Avon and St. Croix Rivers, the region offers a compelling blend of small-town charm and regional connectivity, with easy access to Halifax and the broader Atlantic Canadian economy. Home to a population of just over 19,500, WHRM combines the close-knit feel of its key communities, including Windsor and Hantsport, with the space and character of its surrounding rural landscapes.
West Hants Regional Municipality is a mix of 52 rural and urban communities. It is the first region to welcome you as you arrive in the Annapolis Valley from Halifax.  With more than 1,250 square kms, there is something in every nook and cranny to inspire you to explore more and linger longer. Help Shape the Future of Hospice and Palliative Care on Nova Scotia’s South Shore

The South Shore Hospice Palliative Care Society (SSHPCS) is a community‑founded, donor‑supported charitable organization advancing compassionate, accessible end‑of‑life care for residents of Lunenburg and Queens Counties. Through the generosity of donors and in partnership with the Nova Scotia Health Palliative Care Services team, they support and promote compassionate care for individuals and their loved ones who are living with a life-limiting illness.

Through sustained grassroots leadership and community support, SSHPCS has played a pivotal role in improving access to palliative and hospice care, most notably securing five designated hospice beds at Fishermen’s Memorial Hospital. 

SSHPCS is now entering a pivotal new chapter and is focused on expanding palliative and hospice bed capacity across the South Shore and advancing toward a future freestanding hospice facility, a transformational opportunity that will reshape end‑of‑life care in the region. To lead this next phase, SSHPCS is seeking its first Executive Director.

Help Shape One of Canada’s Fastest‑Growing Regions

Halifax Regional Municipality (HRM) is a vibrant, diverse, and forward‑looking municipality that serves as the economic, cultural, and educational hub of Atlantic Canada. Home to more than half a million residents across over 200 communities, the Halifax region combines urban energy, suburban growth, rural character, and over 400 km of coastline to create an exceptional quality of life. HRM is experiencing one of the most sustained periods of population and development growth in its history.

As the region evolves toward a future of greater density, mobility, and complexity, the importance of thoughtful, credible planning leadership has never been greater.

HRM is now seeking its next Executive Director, Planning and Development. This is one of the most influential planning leadership roles in Canada. Planning decisions being made today will have generational impact on housing affordability, mobility, infrastructure investment, environmental sustainability, and community well being.

Representing top brands like Ponsse, Hyundai, JCB, Rotobec, and Fuchs, and employing over 150 people, the company offers full-service support and operator training. A.L.P.A. continues to be recognized for its leadership, receiving major government investment and actively supporting workforce development and education initiatives.

This is a unique opportunity to play a central role in strengthening and advancing the film and television industry in Newfoundland and Labrador.

The Director’s Guild of Canada represents creative and logistical professionals working in the film, television, and digital media industries across the country. The NLDGC advocates for its members by negotiating and administering collective agreements, supporting safe and fair working conditions, and promoting a strong, competitive production environment within the province. 

The Role 

The NLDGC is seeking a capable and collaborative Business Agent to join our provincial office. Collaborating with national DGC staff, the Business Agent is responsible for managing collective agreements, representing members, and supporting labour relations activities within the jurisdiction of the NLDGC. title: Senior Field Service Representative
description:

Senior Field Service Representative

Senior Field Service Representative

NEW CAREER OPPORTUNITY




Build your career with Kraken Robotics. Make a difference to others. If innovation drives you, and you want a career that makes a difference to the world, helping humanity overcome the challenges in our oceans – safely, efficiently, and sustainably – then come join us at Kraken Robotics.

 

Kraken Robotics is currently recruiting for a Senior Field Service Representative at our office in Mount Pearl, NL. 




The Senior Field Service Representative, reporting to the Service Manager, will build comprehensive knowledge across Krakens’ product line to provide support in the operation, maintenance and repair of Kraken products. This will be a Hybrid position split between office/workshop responsibilities and Field Service engagements. Approximately 50% Field based/50% office based including a mix of Offshore & Nearshore Field Service engagements.

Roles and Responsibilities

Field responsibilities include:

Field Service Calls - Leading field teams in the execution of field support engagements, both scheduled planned maintenance activities and corrective unplanned
Product Demonstrations - execution of product demonstrations, including internal initiatives and customer-led demonstrations such as Naval exercises. Includes installation, operation and maintenance of equipment, execution of mission objectives, reporting.
Operations Support

description:

Calibration Technician

NEW CAREER OPPORTUNITY




Build your career with Kraken Robotics. Make a difference to others. If innovation drives you, and you want a career that makes a difference to the world, helping humanity overcome the challenges in our oceans – safely, efficiently, and sustainably – then come join us at Kraken Robotics.

 

Kraken Robotics is currently seeking a Calibration Technician to join our team in Longmont, CO.




The Calibration Specialist will support the build, calibration, testing and maintenance of Lidar sensor systems. This role is critical to ensuring high quality sensor performance through precise optical alignments, calibration processes, and system level testing. The ideal candidate combines strong technical aptitude with careful execution, thrives in a lab environment and is comfortable working with optics, electronics, and software tools. 

Roles and Responsibilities

Execute full sensor build, calibration and refurbishment workflows following established procedures and flow charts 
Perform optical assembly, alignment, and testing of LiDAR systems and conduct calibration processes, run system integration testing 
Perform incoming inspection on lasers, detectors, scanners and other LiDar components
Diagnose and troubleshoot LiDAR systems in a lab and remotely
Use lab equipment such as oscilloscopes, multimeters and power meters
Troubleshoot communication systems (RS-232

Build your career with Kraken Robotics. Make a difference to others. If innovation drives you, and you want a career that makes a difference to the world, helping humanity overcome the challenges in our oceans – safely, efficiently, and sustainably – then come join us at Kraken Robotics.

 

Kraken Robotics is currently seeking a Subsea LiDAR Specialist to join our team. 




Within Kraken Robotics, the Subsea LiDAR Specialist is a critical technical and client-facing role responsible for delivering high-precision subsea laser scanning solutions across global offshore projects. This position combines offshore operations, advanced data processing, and client engagement to support the successful execution of complex survey and inspection scopes.

The Subsea LiDAR Specialist plays a key role in advancing subsea LiDAR adoption, serving as both a technical expert and field leader in a rapidly growing market.

Plan and execute subsea LiDAR survey projects both offshore and onshore

title: Fachlagerist (m/w/d)
description:
Welcome to the Kraken Robotics Career Center

Fachlagerist (m/w/d)

Fachlagerist (m/w/d)

NEW CAREER OPPORTUNITY

Kraken Robotics 

DEINE KARRIERE BEI KRAKEN
Mit unseren rund 90 Mitarbeiter*innen entwickeln wir, die Kraken Power GmbH, drucktolerante, hochdichte und umweltfreundliche Batterien, die Unterwasserfahrzeuge und -roboter antreiben und in der Unterwasser- und Tiefseeforschung eingesetzt werden.

Kraken Power am Standort Rostock wurde 2016 gegründet und ist Teil der international agierenden Kraken Robotics Inc. mit Sitz in Kanada. Damit bieten wir einzigartige Karrieremöglichkeiten bei einem international anerkannten Marktführer im Bereich der Meerestechnologie.
Neben Kanada und Rostock sind unsere Teams an Standorten auf der ganzen Welt tätig, darunter in den USA, Brasilien, Dänemark und Großbritannien.

Für unseren Standort Rostock suchen wir
Mechatroniker/Elektroniker (m/w/d)




Aufgaben:

Warenannahme sowie Kontrolle der Lieferungen
Ein- und Auslagerung von Waren
Kommissionierung und Verpackung von Aufträgen
Durchführung von Bestandskontrollen und Inventuren
Bedienung von Flurförderzeugen (z. B. Gabelstapler)
Sicherstellung von Ordnung und Sauberkeit im Lagerbereich
Unterstützung bei der Optimierung interner Lagerprozesse




Qualifikation:

Ausbildung oder Weiterbildung im im Bereich Lagerlogistik oder vergleichbare Berufserfahrung
Staplerschein wünschenswert
Teamfähigkeit und körperliche Belastbarkeit
Gute Deutschkenntnisse

title: Intermediate Electrical Engineer
description:
Welcome to the Kraken Robotics Career Center

Intermediate Electrical Engineer

Intermediate Electrical Engineer

NEW CAREER OPPORTUNITY




Build your career with Kraken Robotics. Make a difference to others. If innovation drives you, and you want a career that makes a difference to the world, helping humanity overcome the challenges in our oceans – safely, efficiently, and sustainably – then come join us at Kraken Robotics.

 

📍 Mount Pearl, NL (Remote within Canada considered with future transfer to Mount Pearl)




Build technology that operates where few others can—deep ocean environments.




At Kraken Robotics, we design advanced subsea imaging, battery, and robotic systems used around the world. We’re growing our engineering team and looking for an Intermediate Electrical Engineer to contribute to the design of mission-critical electronics for next-generation ocean technologies.




You’ll report to the Engineering Manager, Newfoundland.




What You’ll Work On

Design of mixed-signal PCBs and embedded electrical systems for subsea robotics and sensing platforms
Architect electrical systems spanning power, signal integrity, and embedded control
Develop and validate designs for extreme environments (pressure, temperature, EMI)
Collaborate closely with mechanical, firmware, and systems engineers to deliver fully integrated products
Guide products from prototype → production → field deployment
Mentor junior engineers and co-op students
Apply required codes and standards

Welcome to the Kraken Robotics Career Center

Senior Project Manager

Senior Project Manager

NEW CAREER OPPORTUNITY




Build your career with Kraken Robotics. Make a difference to others. If innovation drives you, and you want a career that makes a difference to the world, helping humanity overcome the challenges in our oceans – safely, efficiently, and sustainably – then come join us at Kraken Robotics.

 

Kraken Robotics is currently recruiting for a Project Manager to join our Mount Pearl, Newfoundland and Dartmouth, Nova Scotia offices.

 

We are seeking a proactive Senior Project Manager with expertise in industrial and manufacturing projects. This role is responsible for ensuring customer satisfaction through the delivery of high-quality products that meet performance and schedule expectations. 

The Senior Project Manager will lead, manage, coordinate, and report on multiple projects and assemble and guide cross functional project teams with a strong focus on manufacturing execution, outsourced production strategies, and industrial process integration.

The ideal candidate brings strong experience in manufacturing and finance, can adapt quickly to evolving business needs, thrives in a fast-paced, deadline-driven team environment, and is motivated to contribute to the strategic direction of future Kraken operations and products.




ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

Develop and maintain project business plans and coordinate each project through a stage gate process.

title: Payroll & People Analytics Coordinator
description:
Welcome to the Kraken Robotics Career Center

Payroll & People Analytics Coordinator

Payroll & People Analytics Coordinator

NEW CAREER OPPORTUNITY




Build your career with Kraken Robotics. Make a difference to others. If innovation drives you, and you want a career that makes a difference to the world, helping humanity overcome the challenges in our oceans – safely, efficiently, and sustainably – then come join us at Kraken Robotics.

 

Kraken Robotics is currently seeking a Payroll & People Analytics Coordinator to join our team in Mount Pearl, NL. 




This role sits at the intersection of People & Culture (P&C) operations and data, ensuring employees are paid correctly, group benefits programs and other perks run smoothly, and leadership has access to meaningful workforce analytics to support decision-making.




Roles and Responsibilities:

Payroll Administration

Support end-to-end global payroll on a regular cycle of semi-monthly across North Americal and monthly across the United Kingdom
Ensure payroll accuracy, including salaries and statutory deductions
Maintain payroll records and ensure compliance with local tax and employment regulations
Liaise with external payroll providers and internal finance teams
Support audits and reporting requirements

Benefits Administration

Support the employee benefits programs (health, retirement, insurance, wellness)
Support employee enrollments, changes, and terminations in benefits systems
Act as a point of contact for employ

title: Senior Full Stack Engineer, Healthcare
description:
Senior Full Stack Engineer, Healthcare
Location

USA; Brooklyn, NYC

Employment Type

Full time

Location Type

Remote

Department

Engineering

OverviewApplication

About Us

Propel is on a mission to build technology that strengthens the social safety net. We’re a passionate team of ~100 Propellers who envision a future where every American has the tools and resources they need to thrive.

The Role

Are you a Senior Fullstack Engineer looking to build 0→1 products at a mission-driven company? Do you enjoy creating consumer-grade experiences and thrive in high-autonomy startup environments?

We’re looking for a motivated Senior Fullstack Engineer to join Propel and help build new Medicare and Medicaid products from the ground up. In this role, you’ll work end-to-end across frontend and backend systems to develop user experiences that help people understand coverage options, surface savings opportunities, and enroll in healthcare plans. 

You’ll collaborate closely with product and design, and help shape technical architecture, internal tooling, and scalable integrations with healthcare and government systems.

What we offer:

An engineering culture that is humble, motivated, and collaborative 

Consistent knowledge sharing through projects or bugs demos, Lunch & Learn presentations, and documentation

A people-first approach to management where we value engineers’ voices, support career development, and offer opportunities for technical growth that interest you

Meaningful w

title: Office & People Operations Intern (With Full-time Potential)
description:
Office & People Operations Intern (With Full-time Potential)
Location

Brooklyn, NYC

Employment Type

Intern

Location Type

On-site

Department

G&A

Deadline to Apply

May 29, 2026 at 6:30 PM GMT-2:30

Compensation
$25 – $30 per hour
OverviewApplication
About Us

Propel builds technology that strengthens the social safety net in America. We envision a future where every American has the tools and resources they need to thrive.

The safety net's strength depends not just on funding, but delivery. Propel exists to dramatically improve how government benefits are delivered. Managing SNAP benefits, picking a D-SNP plan, or protecting against EBT theft should feel as seamless as Apple Pay or Netflix.

Governments struggle to build modern, scalable user experiences. We don't.

Over 5.5 million Americans trust Propel today. We've grown revenue 35% YoY and are reinventing entire benefit categories, including Medicare, around the real needs of low-income Americans. We're building a high-growth, high-ceiling, sustainable for-profit company where impact drives revenue.

About the Role

We're hiring a part-time Office & People Operations Intern to help keep our Brooklyn office running and our team well-supported. This role sits at the intersection of people ops, facilities, and administrative coordination - the kind of work that makes everything else easier.

Our current office manager is moving on to a new opportunity, and we're restructuring the responsibilities of the team and the

title: Medicare Advisor
description:
Medicare Advisor
Location

USA; Brooklyn, NYC

Employment Type

Full time

Location Type

Remote

Department

New Ventures

OverviewApplication
About Us

Propel builds technology that strengthens the social safety net in America. We envision a future where every American has the tools and resources they need to thrive.

The safety net’s strength depends not just on funding, but delivery. Propel exists to dramatically improve how government benefits are delivered. Managing SNAP benefits, picking a D-SNP plan, or protecting against EBT theft should feel as seamless as Apple Pay or Netflix.

Governments struggle to build modern, scalable user experiences. We don’t.

Over 5.5 million Americans trust Propel today. We’ve grown revenue 35% YoY and are reinventing entire benefit categories, including Medicare, around the real needs of low-income Americans. We’re building a high-growth, high-ceiling, sustainable for-profit company where impact drives revenue.

About the Role

Propel serves approximately 1M users who are Medicare eligible, most of whom are also Medicaid eligible. We are building the first-ever app-based brokerage focused on helping "duals" (Americans on Medicare and Medicaid) enroll in healthcare plans. We specialize in Dual Eligible Special Needs Plans (D-SNPs) and are expanding to 30 states in 2026, with plans to move into larger Medicare markets over the next 24 months. As a Medicare Advisor, you will be a trusted expert, helping users compare Medicare plans, enroll

title: Senior Brokerage Operations Associate
description:
Senior Brokerage Operations Associate
Location

USA; Brooklyn, NYC

Employment Type

Full time

Location Type

Remote

Department

New Ventures

OverviewApplication
About Us

Propel is on a mission to empower low-income Americans by simplifying access to government benefits with modern technology. We're a passionate team of 100 Propellers - all working to help our users get through the month, every month.

The Role

Propel owns Willoughby, a licensed entity building the first app-based digital Medicare brokerage, initially focused on dual Medicare-Medicaid users.

We’re looking for a Senior Associate to help build and scale the operational foundation of our business. This is a highly cross-functional, individual-contributor role focused on automation, operations, analytics, and partner execution.

You’ll work closely with Product, Engineering, Data, Compliance, and GTM to reduce manual work, improve reliability, and enable Propel to scale into new markets and partnerships.

The core of what you’ll do
Back Office Automation

Build and own internal-facing tools that automate core backoffice and post-enrollment workflows

Identify high-impact opportunities to reduce manual effort, improve accuracy, and increase operational efficiency

Partner with Product and Engineering to scope, ship, and iterate quickly on automation

Operations & Partnerships

Support and increasingly own key operational partner relationships, including FMOs

Manage carrier contracting and onboarding to enab

title: Growth & Insights Lead, Public Sector
description:
Growth & Insights Lead, Public Sector
Location

USA

Employment Type

Full time

Location Type

Remote

Department

Public Partnerships and Policy

Compensation
Base Salary $150K – $180K • Offers Equity
OverviewApplication

About Us

Propel is on a mission to empower low-income Americans by simplifying access to government benefits with modern technology. We're a passionate team of 100 Propellers - all working to help our users get through the month, every month.

The Role

Millions of Americans navigate a social safety net that too often works against them - confusing enrollment, broken eligibility systems, policies that never reach the people they're meant to serve. Propel exists to change that. And increasingly, the path to change runs through the states that administer these programs.

When Propel builds credible relationships with state agencies, we don't just grow the business - we create direct pathways to improving life for the people we serve. A state that understands how its users are falling through the cracks can actually fix it. But that requires state leaders to know what Propel sees and trust the source.

The Growth & Insights Lead will make that possible by reaching the right agency decision-makers, producing insights grounded in real user experience, and developing messaging that earns trust in a complex public sector environment. This is a full-cycle role: the right person will generate the idea, pull the data, and take it all the way to market themselves. S

title: Senior Solutions Architect, Government Partnerships
description:
Senior Solutions Architect, Government Partnerships
Location

USA

Employment Type

Full time

Location Type

Remote

Department

Public Partnerships and Policy

Compensation
Base Salary $160K – $190K • Offers Equity
OverviewApplication

About Us

Propel is on a mission to empower low-income Americans by simplifying access to government benefits with modern technology. We're a passionate team of 100 Propellers - all working to help our users get through the month, every month.

About the Role

We're looking for a Solutions Architect to bridge Propel's Government Partnerships team and our Product & Engineering organization - translating state partners' needs into product strategy, and then owning the delivery of that work with states. This role has the opportunity to shape the way our product evolves with deeper government partnerships and increase the value we’re able to deliver to both users and government partners.

This is a hands-on IC role. You'll be the person writing the scoping doc, building the dashboard, sitting in the state meeting, configuring the campaign, and running the evaluation. You own both strategy and execution end to end. Over time, as this work scales, the role has potential to grow into a leadership position — but today we need someone who is excited about the execution.

You'll work across the state partnership funnel, from early relationship-building through campaign delivery. You should be the kind of person who can walk into a meeting with a state
